steps.title: Use the area scale factor
steps.content: For similar figures, lengths scale by the linear scale factor, but areas scale by the square of that factor. A side-length scale factor of $5$ gives an area scale factor of $5^2=25$.

steps.title: Identify scale factor and apply the area scaling rule
steps.content: Each side of UVWX is 5 times the corresponding side of QRST, giving a linear scale factor of $k = 5$. For similar figures, areas scale by $k^2$, so the area scale factor is $5^2 = 25$.

steps.title: Calculate the area of UVWX
steps.content: $A_{\text{UVWX}} = 75 \times 25 = 1,875$ square units. That confirms D is correct.
